precedents.title: Northgate Mutual SLA dispute (2024)
precedents.summary: Northgate Mutual disputed credit calculations after INC-101, arguing degraded performance should count as full downtime. Their counsel cited our own marketing line 'always on, always bright.'
precedents.outcome: Settled: 25% goodwill credit beyond the automatic SLA credit, no admission of breach. Marketing lines are now reviewed by legal because they get quoted back to us in disputes.

precedents.title: Billing decimal class action (Perkins v. Lumina Cloud, 2025-2026)
precedents.summary: Class action following INC-105's 100x invoices, alleging emotional distress and short-term credit-line impacts even though no cards were charged.
precedents.outcome: Settled for $1.4M with no admission of wrongdoing. Key lesson: an early support macro saying 'we guarantee full compensation' was Exhibit A. Never promise compensation before finance sign-off.

precedents.title: FTC informal inquiry re: LuminaCoin blog post (2022)
precedents.summary: The FTC asked informal questions about the 2021 'LuminaCoin' blog post, specifically whether phrases implying 'guaranteed returns' constituted an unregistered securities promotion.
precedents.outcome: Closed with no action after the post was retracted with a correction note and the board adopted resolution 2022-07 prohibiting any token, coin, or blockchain product. Any new statement about LuminaCoin must be unambiguous that no token exists or is planned.

precedents.title: Nimbus Dynamics trademark settlement (2025)
precedents.summary: Nimbus Dynamics claimed our 'Beacon' branding infringed their 'BeaconIQ' mark. Cross-claims followed over their 'Glowline' product name.
precedents.outcome: Settled: both parties keep their marks, plus a mutual non-disparagement clause. Public statements must not attack Nimbus Dynamics — even when discussing their logo appearing in our own advertising.

precedents.title: Llama-related workplace accommodation grievance (2024)
precedents.summary: An employee grievance alleged 'llama-related workplace distraction' and one incident of expectoration adjacent to (not upon) the complainant. Raised interplay between ESA accommodation law and coworker comfort.
precedents.outcome: Resolved internally: Biscuit's visits restricted to Tuesdays 2-4 pm, third floor only, with designated llama-free floors. Establishes that Biscuit's HQ presence is a lawful, documented accommodation — but confers no access rights to any other facility.

precedents.title: EU data protection authority correspondence re: INC-103 (2025)
precedents.summary: A European DPA requested details on the Glowworm staging-log scare, focusing on notification timeliness and log-retention practices.
precedents.outcome: Closed without penalty. Lumina's 72-hour proactive notification was cited favorably. Standing commitment: notify affected customers within 72 hours of any suspected data-handling issue, even before forensics conclude.

precedents.title: FAA warning letter re: AirDrop fountain landing (2026)
precedents.summary: Following INC-108, the FAA issued a warning letter regarding autonomous waypoint operations near people during the press demo.
precedents.outcome: Corrective plan accepted: geofencing update and mandatory two-person waypoint review. A second deviation event within 24 months risks certificate action against the pilot program — repeat incidents must be self-reported promptly and the program voluntarily paused.

precedents.title: Fernwood Logistics support-SLA arbitration (2025)
precedents.summary: Fernwood Logistics sought contract termination after repeated slow first responses on enterprise tickets, arguing automated acknowledgments were being counted as 'responses.'
precedents.outcome: Arbitrator ruled acknowledgments do not constitute meaningful response; Support Policy SUP-12 was rewritten accordingly. Fernwood stayed after receiving support-fee credits. Directly relevant to any incident involving Lumi auto-replies.

banned_phrases: we failed to exercise reasonable care
approved_apology_language: we fell short of the standard you deserve
this is not the experience we want anyone to have with lumina cloud
we understand the frustration, and we're acting on it
we hold ourselves to a higher standard than what you saw today
we're making it right, starting today
we know we have trust to rebuild, and we intend to earn it
